_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates batch, state tier: HD-27 Atoosa Reaser (D), HD-28 David Reid (D), HD-29 Marty Martinez (D), HD-30 John McAuliff (D), HD-31 Delores Riley Oates (R). Evidence-first finalization from primary roll-call records (lis.virginia.gov vote-details, legacy LIS roll calls), advocacy scorecards/endorsements (ReproRising, Choice Tracker, Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA, Giffords, Everytown), and direct candidate statements. The four Loudoun/Fauquier Democrats show uniformly progressive documented records (co-patron/support of the reproductive-freedom and same-sex-marriage constitutional amendments, gun-control votes/endorsements) and no documented conservative positions. Oates (R) has the strongest record: individual LIS roll-call NAY votes on both amendments (did NOT cross party like Tara Durant did), plus chief-patron conservative bills. McAuliff is a 2026 freshman whose individual roll calls were blocked from automated retrieval, so only his HARD personal statements were scored; his marriage-amendment vote was left null (not inferred from party).

profile.religion: No explicit personal faith affiliation documented; described as the first woman of Muslim heritage / first Iranian-American elected to VA state office, with campaign content on Ramadan (heritage framing, not a stated denomination).
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-27 (eastern Loudoun); sworn in Jan 10, 2024, re-elected Nov 4, 2025. Attorney (J.D., George Mason); B.A. James Madison University. Born in Tehran, fled the revolution as a child, first-generation immigrant and former English-language learner. Served on the Loudoun County School Board 2020–2023 (twice Vice Chair).

atoosa-reaser.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-27 (eastern Loudoun), sitting since Jan 2024. [2026-06-02 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Documented progressive record: co-patron of HJ1 (reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment) and of HB609/HB1539 (contraception right + abortion shield law); endorsed by REPRO Rising VA PAC and Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA; co-patron of HB174 (marriage lawful regardless of sex) and publicly backs the marriage-equality constitutional amendment; co-patron of the 2024 assault-weapons ban (HB2), auto-sear ban (HB22), and firearm-locking-device mandate (HB158), endorsed by Everytown and Giffords; chief patron of HB1490/HB2056 expanding in-person absentee voting (attempted to override Youngkin's veto). No conservative cross-party deviation found on life, marriage, guns, or elections. Cells with no public record (Christian liberty, federal overreach, border/immigration, several sub-topics) left null — not inferred from party.

0.note: Co-patron of HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; REPRO Rising-endorsed 'champion'; public statement supporting a woman's reproductive choice — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Co-patron of HB609 (right to contraception) and HB1539 (abortion shield law) — opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

0.note: Co-patron of HB174 (marriage lawful regardless of sex/gender) and publicly backs the constitutional marriage-equality amendment — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Supports constitutional protection for same-sex marriage.

2.note: Chief patron of HB1490/HB2056 expanding in-person absentee voting and locality satellite-office authority — opposite of single-day in-person voting.

3.note: Expanded absentee/early-voting access (attempted to override the governor's veto of HB2056) — opposite of opposing mass mail-in/early voting.

1.note: Co-patron of HB2 (2024 assault-weapons & magazine ban), HB22 (auto-sear/trigger-activator ban), and HB158 (mandatory firearm locking device); endorsed by Everytown and Giffords.

profile.religion: United Methodist; attends Arcola United Methodist Church (Loudoun County).
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-28 (eastern Loudoun: Ashburn/Broadlands); first elected 2017, in office since Jan 2018 (formerly HD-32), re-elected 2023 and 2025. 23-year U.S. Navy Reserve Naval Intelligence Officer, retired as Commander (O-5); Master's in Strategic Intelligence. Tech-sector professional. House Appropriations; Vice Chair, Transportation; Privileges & Elections.

david-reid.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-28 (eastern Loudoun), sitting since 2018. [2026-06-02 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Documented progressive record: ReproRising A+/A and Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA-endorsed, listed as a patron of abortion-access legislation, public statement that 'only a woman and her doctor' should decide; co-patron of the marriage-equality constitutional amendment (per iVoterGuide) and endorsed by LGBT Democrats of Virginia; CONFIRMED individual roll-call YEA on HB2 (2024 assault-weapons ban, legacy LIS HV1385) and YEA on HB1 (2024 minimum wage, HV0202); Giffords-endorsed. No conservative cross-party deviation found. Cells with no public record left null — not inferred from party. (Disambiguation: NOT John Reid, the Republican 2025 LG nominee.)

0.note: ReproRising A+/A 'champion'; Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA-endorsed; public statement that 'only a woman and her doctor' should make health decisions — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Listed as a patron of legislation to expand abortion access — opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

0.note: Co-patron of the marriage-equality constitutional amendment; endorsed by LGBT Democrats of Virginia — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Supports codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.
CONFIRMED individual roll-call YEA on HB2 (2024) — purchase/sale/transfer of assault firearms and certain magazines prohibited; Giffords-endorsed.

profile.religion: Catholic (per the official Virginia House of Delegates member biography).
profile.background: Fernando J. 'Marty' Martinez, VA Delegate HD-29 (Town of Leesburg/Lansdowne, Loudoun); sworn in Jan 10, 2024, re-elected Nov 4, 2025. U.S. Air Force veteran (1970–1978, Staff Sergeant, Vietnam era). ~20 years on Leesburg Town Council (Vice Mayor 2019–2023). Retired FAA-supporting government contractor. B.S. Computer Science + Economics, Portland State.

marty-martinez.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-29 (Leesburg/Loudoun), sitting since Jan 2024. [2026-06-02 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Documented progressive record: Choice Tracker 'Pro-Choice Champion' (sponsored contraception-right and abortion-provider-shield bills, voted against public-abortion-funding restrictions), co-patron/support of HJ1 reproductive-freedom amendment, endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA and NOW; recorded YES on HJ9 (same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment); endorsed by Giffords and Everytown (Giffords cites his pledge to 'close gun-ownership loopholes' and strengthen background checks). Thin chief-patron record (mostly off-scorecard). No conservative cross-party deviation found. Cells with no public record left null — not inferred from party.

0.note: Choice Tracker 'Pro-Choice Champion'; co-patron/support of HJ1 reproductive-freedom amendment; Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA and NOW-endorsed — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Sponsored contraception-right (HB1716/HB2371) and abortion-provider shield (HB519) bills; voted against public-abortion-funding restrictions — opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.

0.note: Recorded YES on HJ9, the constitutional amendment to repeal Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Supports codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (YES on HJ9).

Endorsed by Giffords and Everytown; Giffords cites his pledge to 'close gun-ownership loopholes, strengthen background checks, and encourage safe storage' — opposite of opposing such gun-control measures.

profile.religion: No religious affiliation documented (10th-generation descendant of Mayflower passenger Mary Chilton — heritage, not a stated faith).
profile.background: John Chilton McAuliff, VA Delegate HD-30 (northern Fauquier/western Loudoun); first elected Nov 4, 2025, sworn in Jan 14, 2026 — a freshman in his first (2026) session; flipped the seat from GOP incumbent Geary Higgins. B.A. Leadership Studies, University of Richmond. Former chief of staff to Del. David Reid; USDA Rural Development; White House Office of Clean Energy Innovation (IRA implementation). Founded Chilton House Suites B&B in Warrenton.

john-mcauliff.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-30 (Fauquier/western Loudoun), 2026 freshman. [2026-06-02 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Documented progressive positions where evidence exists: Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA and NOW-endorsed with a direct statement pledging to 'vote to enshrine reproductive rights in the Virginia Constitution in 2026'; direct statement supporting 'universal background checks and red-flag laws.' EVIDENCE NOTE: as a 2026 freshman, his individual roll calls (incl. the HJ3 marriage amendment) could not be retrieved from automated sources (LIS/VPAP blocked), so the marriage cells were left NULL rather than inferred from the party-line vote — only his HARD personal statements were scored. A future pass with VPAP access can upgrade. No conservative position found.

0.note: Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA and NOW-endorsed; direct statement pledging to 'vote to enshrine reproductive rights in the Virginia Constitution in 2026' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-abortion-access (PPAV/NOW-endorsed; pledged to enshrine reproductive rights) — opposite of abortion-abolition legislation.
Direct campaign statement supporting 'universal background checks and red-flag laws' — opposite of opposing red-flag laws.

profile.religion: No denomination publicly named; B.A. in Government from Regent University (a Christian university) and bio states she 'volunteers in various churches' — faith markers, not a stated affiliation.
profile.background: Delores Riley Oates, VA Delegate HD-31 (Warren/Clarke/Frederick, Shenandoah Valley); took office Jan 10, 2024, re-elected Nov 4, 2025. Born in Front Royal; Warren County resident. A.A.S. Lord Fairfax CC; B.A. Government, Regent University (2017). VP at Foxhound Federal / senior technical recruiter. Former Warren County Board of Supervisors (2019–2023).

delores-riley-oates.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-31 (Shenandoah Valley), sitting since Jan 2024. [2026-06-02 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly conservative HARD record verified from individual LIS roll-call vote records (her personal Yea/Nay codes, 2025 SessionID 57 + 2026 SessionID 59) plus chief-patron bills and direct statements. CROSS-PARTY CHECK: she did NOT cross over (unlike Tara Durant) — voted NAY on the same-sex-marriage amendment (HJ9 2025, HJ3 2026) and NAY on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1 2025+2026). Pro-life ('human life begins at conception'); chief patron of women's-sports/biological-sex bills (HB1809/HB1242); supports school choice + homeschool tax credits; YEA on National Day of Prayer (SJ59) and religious accommodations (HB131); tax-relief and anti-minimum-wage votes; chief patron of voter-ID tightening (HB2402/HB1213); pro-police and anti-cashless-bail votes; opposed anti-ICE-cooperation bills (HB1441); consistent pro-2A NAY votes on the entire gun-control package. Thin cells (federal overreach, several sub-topics) left null — credit held to documented votes, not inferred from party.

0.note: Direct statement: 'Human life begins at conception'; voted NAY on HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (2025 first reference and 2026 second reference).
Voted NAY on HJ9 (2025) / HJ3 (2026), the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment — among the GOP 'no' bloc, did NOT cross over.

1.note: Voted NAY on the constitutional amendment to enshrine same-sex marriage (and its Senate companions SJ249/SJ3).

2.note: Chief patron of HB1809 (2025)/HB1242 (2026) restricting K-12 and college women's-sports participation to biological sex — affirms immutable biological sex.

4.note: Chief patron of the women's-sports/biological-sex bill — opposes promotion of gender ideology in state-funded schools.

0.note: Direct statement supporting school choice, vouchers, and tax credits for homeschooling families; chief patron HB2620 making school exams available to satisfy compulsory-attendance (homeschool/alternative-ed access).

2.note: Statement that 'parents have a fundamental right to choose what students are taught'; chief patron of the women's-sports/biological-sex bill opposing gender-ideology in schools.

1.note: Voted YEA on HB131 (2026), religious accommodations at public colleges; statement defending religious-practice protections under Virginia's Statute of Religious Freedom.

3.note: Voted YEA on SJ59 (2026) National Day of Prayer and HB2302 (2025) real-property tax exemption for religious buildings — supports prayer/religious presence in public bodies.

2.note: Voted YEA on income-tax-relief bills (HB1997/HB2549 2025; HB716/HB958 2026) and NAY on every minimum-wage increase and public-sector collective-bargaining expansion; chief patron of car-tax relief (HB1205/HB1281); pledges to reduce taxes — fiscal-restraint posture.

1.note: Chief patron of HB2402 (2025)/HB1213 (2026) tightening voter-ID 'affirmation statement' rules; statement supporting 'common sense voter identification protections.'

3.note: Voted NAY on absentee-ballot-deadline extensions (SB760 2025; HB82/SB58 2026) — opposes expansion of mass mail-in/absentee voting.

0.note: Pro-police record: voted NAY on law-enforcement civilian-oversight disclosure (HB1476) and LE 'duty to render aid' civil-liability bills (HB273); YEA on penalty enhancements protecting correctional officers (HB294).

1.note: Voted NAY on bills loosening pretrial release/bail conditions (HB2217 2025; HB357/HB127 2026) — opposes cashless-bail/soft-on-crime measures.

2.note: Voted YEA on penalty enhancements (escape from jail HB2406; critical-infrastructure trespass) and fentanyl/drug-death accountability (involuntary manslaughter for drug deaths); chief patron of stalking-penalty HB2429 — proportional punishment of evildoers.

Voted NAY on HB1441/SB783 (2026) restricting local law-enforcement cooperation with federal immigration authorities; statement opposing sanctuary-city policies and in-state tuition for undocumented immigrants.

1.note: Consistent pro-2A record: voted NAY on assault-weapons/magazine bans (HB1607 2025; HB217 2026), waiting periods, red-flag/substantial-risk orders, and mandatory-storage bills; chief patron of CCW bill HB2414; statement opposing 'liberal gun grabbers.'
